# Understanding Your Site Pages

Your Faithmade site has two types of pages, and knowing the difference saves a ton of confusion.

## Editable Content Pages

These are pages you build and customize yourself using Page Builder. Your homepage, about page, and any custom pages fall into this category. You have full control over layout, content, images, and design.

## Auto-Generated Pages

These pages are created automatically from your content. When you add sermons, events, groups, or blog posts, Faithmade builds listing pages and detail pages for you. No extra work needed.

For example:
- Every sermon you add automatically gets its own page with the player, notes, and series info
- Your events page automatically lists upcoming events
- Your groups page lists all active groups

## How to Tell Which Is Which

If you can open it in Page Builder (via **Design > Pages**), it's an editable content page. If it's generated from content you entered elsewhere (like sermons or events), it's auto-generated.

## Why This Matters

Want to change how your homepage looks? Head to **Page Builder**. Want to change what shows on your sermon page? Edit the sermon itself under **Content > Sermons**.

**Related:** [Editing Pages with Page Builder](./editing-pages-with-page-builder) · [Adding a New Page](./adding-a-new-page)
